author: JMLee
message:
feat(model): sync model/effort pickers with Claude Code and add Ultracode

Update pickers to match the latest Claude Code model-config docs:
- Add the "fable" model alias (Claude Fable 5) with localized
  descriptions, and update the "best" alias description to reflect
  that it resolves to Fable 5 where available
- Recognize the Fable family in formatModelId so raw IDs like
  claude-fable-5 render as "Fable 5"
- Refresh effort level descriptions: high is the default on current
  models, xhigh is deeper reasoning (default on Opus 4.7)
- Fix the max effort note in the picker sheet ("Opus 4.6 only" is
  outdated; max is session-only on all effort-capable models)

Add a session-only Ultracode option to the chat effort pickers
(toolbar menu, /effort sheet, and /effort command). Ultracode is a
Claude Code setting rather than an effort level, so it is delivered
as "ultracode": true merged into the hook settings file passed via
--settings, and the --effort flag is omitted. It is intentionally
excluded from the Default Effort Level setting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: JMLee
message: chore(release): v1.3.9 (build 37)

author: JMLee
message:
fix(statusbar): restore session stats on switch and app activation

- Replace scenePhase with NSApplication.didBecomeActiveNotification for
  reliable rate-limit refresh when switching back from another app
- Auto-restore last session on project load so status bar shows persisted
  stats immediately without requiring a manual session click
- Eagerly update allSessionSummaries in releaseOutgoingSession before the
  async disk save, preventing stale reads when switching back quickly
- Preserve totalDurationMs and contextPercent in reloadSessionSummaries
  merge so FS watcher reloads don't overwrite eagerly-set in-memory values
- Fall back to wall-clock elapsed time when result event omits duration_ms
  (or reports 0), ensuring cumulative duration accumulates correctly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: JMLee
message: chore(release): v1.3.8 (build 36)

author: JMLee
message:
fix(queue): migrate draft queue when stream key changes to real session id

Queued messages are stored under the session key in draftQueues. When a
stream's key was re-keyed from a pending/temp id to the real CLI session
id, only sessionStates moved — draftQueues stayed orphaned under the old
key. As a result a queue built up before switching away was invisible on
return and skipped by background queue processing.

Add migrateDraftState() and call it at both re-key sites (system init and
result) so draftQueues/draftTexts follow the session to its real id. The
queue now survives session switches, keeps showing on return, and keeps
processing in the background.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: JMLee
message:
fix(streaming): prevent background stream from hijacking foreground window

Centralize foreground-ownership into `isForegroundStream(_:in:)` so every
site that gates window mutations uses the same predicate. A stream detached
to the background when the user switches projects must not write to
`window.currentSessionId` or surface error bubbles in the new project.

Previously the `init`-event handler carried `|| window.currentSessionId == nil`,
which treated any detached stream as foreground the moment the window showed
a fresh-chat screen — causing the old project's messages to bleed in and
triggering a cascade of phantom session IDs on each response.

Fixed sites:
- system(init): session-ID update guard
- result: foreground flag used for `currentSessionId` update and error UI
- rateLimitEvent: error bubble gate
- eventCount==0 cleanup: error bubble gate (incomplete migration, now fixed)

Tests added for all three isForegroundStream invariants; test scheme
configured to include ClarcTests target.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: JMLee
message:
fix(sidebar): refresh git status on project tab switch

The perf commit moved path-change git refresh from onChange(of:
projectPath) into .task(id: projectPath). When selectProject batches
its mutations into a single withAnimation(nil) transaction, .task(id:)
fails to re-run, leaving the previous project's git status on screen.
Switching via the session list happened to work because switchToSession
triggered an extra update cycle.

Restore the direct refresh() in onAppear and onChange(of: projectPath),
dropping the fragile .task dependency. The parallel git calls and the
no-loading-flash behavior live in fetchGitStatus/refresh, so all perf
wins are preserved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: JMLee
message: chore(release): v1.3.7 (build 35)

author: JMLee
message:
fix(status): refresh rate limit usage when app becomes active

The status bar rate limit usage only refreshed on initial view load and
when streaming ended, so the values went stale while the app sat idle in
the background. Add a scenePhase .active trigger so usage (and the reset
time) is re-fetched whenever the app returns to the foreground. The
RateLimitService 5-minute cache keeps this from causing extra network
requests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: JMLee
message:
fix(inspector): keep memo's first line clear of the window titlebar

In the right-docked split (showBoth) panel the memo's NSScrollView sits
under the translucent window titlebar, and AppKit's automatic content-inset
adjustment doesn't fire when the scroll view is hosted inside a SwiftUI
VSplitView, so the first line was clipped. MemoScrollView now measures its
own overlap with the window's content-layout area and applies that as a top
content inset — self-correcting to 0 when not under the titlebar (single-tab,
bottom dock). The single-tab tab bar also moves into the layout flow (VStack)
so it no longer overlaps the memo.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: JMLee
message:
feat(inspector): add docking position and split layout for memo/terminal panel

Add a General-settings control to dock the memo/terminal inspector on the
right (default) or at the bottom, plus a "show both" toggle that splits the
panel — memo over terminal on the right, side by side at the bottom — with
draggable resize handles.

Remove the panel title bar: in single-tab mode the tab switcher floats over
the content, and the terminal gets a floating reset button at the bottom
trailing corner; the memo clear action moves to the formatting toolbar.

Restart the terminal in place via a reset trigger instead of recreating the
view, so split divider positions are preserved. Source terminal colors from
the active theme. Localize all new strings across the six supported languages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: JMLee
message:
fix(status-bar): stop zero-duration saves from wiping cumulative time

After a response saved the cumulative durationMs, a follow-up save fired
while the in-memory stream state had been recreated with durationMs=0 (e.g.
after a session switch), and the "stateDur ?? existing" logic persisted that
0 — clobbering the real total. context % was unaffected since it is a latest
value, not an accumulator.

durationMs is monotonic, so persist max(live, previously-saved) in both
saveSession and releaseOutgoingSession instead of letting a transient 0 win.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: JMLee
message:
fix(status-bar): preserve cumulative duration and context % when leaving a session

releaseOutgoingSession built a ChatSession without contextPercent,
totalDurationMs, model, effort, or permissionMode and saved it. Since
saveSession overwrites the whole sidecar meta file, switching away from a
session wiped its persisted status-bar stats, leaving cumulative duration
blank on return (context % recovered only via the 5s CLI re-fetch).

Carry the in-memory state (falling back to the existing summary) into the
outgoing ChatSession so the meta write keeps these fields intact.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: JMLee
message:
fix(chat): remove trailing blank line below assistant bubbles

The last assistant text block reserved a full empty line (Text(" ")) to
keep the markdown renderer's stripped trailing newline from clipping the
final paragraph at the bubble edge. That left a visible gap below short
responses.

Fix the root cause instead: give the rendered text its ideal height via
fixedSize(vertical:), so the final line is never clipped, and drop the
blank-line reservation along with its isLastTextBlock bookkeeping.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: JMLee
message:
feat(status-bar): persist rate-limit usage across app restarts

The 5h/7d rate-limit usage was memory-only in RateLimitService, so the
status bar showed "--" after every app launch until the first API fetch
returned. Persist the last known snapshot in an app-wide store (single
UserDefaults key, not per-session) and seed the in-memory cache from it
on init so values appear immediately.

- Make RateLimitUsage Codable for serialization
- Load persisted snapshot in init (cachedAt left nil for revalidation)
- Save on each successful fetch; existing 5-min TTL keeps API calls bounded
  while per-response refresh continues via StatusLineView

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: JMLee
message:
revert(theme): remove additional themes and theme-aware toolbar styling

Drop the six extra themes (Dracula, Nord, Monokai, One Dark, Rose,
Latte) and the theme-aware window toolbar styling introduced in
a3c529b, keeping only the original six themes. The session metadata
changes from that commit are retained.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: JMLee
message:
feat(status-bar): persist context % and cumulative duration per session

Status-bar context usage and cumulative response time were memory-only
and only showed up after a chat response. Persist both in the session
sidecar (SessionMetaStore) and restore them on session open so they
appear immediately without waiting for a new response.

- Add contextPercent / totalDurationMs to Meta, ChatSession, Summary
- Save stats on session save; patch context % async after CLI fetch
- Restore into SessionStreamState on session switch
- Source the status-bar duration from the persisted cumulative stat

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: JMLee
message:
fix(input): preserve draft when navigating message history

Stepping into message history with the up arrow overwrote the in-progress
input, and the down arrow cleared it to empty when stepping back out. Save
the draft on entering history and restore it on exit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
